description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a distinctive arched, cathedral-style wooden cabinet. The front features a circular speaker grille with decorative horizontal slats and a smaller, round tuning window below it, both set into a dark, polished wood face. Two control knobs are visible on the lower front, likely for tuning and volume adjustment, and a fabric-covered power cord with a two-prong plug extends from the back. The sides and back are smooth, with a rich, dark wood grain and no additional features except for the cord exit. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century radios, combining both functional and ornamental elements. The object appears solid and well-crafted, with a glossy finish that highlights the wood's texture.
description: A vintage, arched wooden radio receiver with a circular speaker grille, tuning window, and two control knobs on the front, finished in dark polished wood. A fabric-covered power cord extends from the back.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched shape, circular speaker grille with decorative slats, tuning window, two control knobs, and the power cord extending to the right.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the smooth, curved wooden surface and the power cord trailing away from the back.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing a plain, dark wooden back panel with the power cord emerging from the lower center.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the curved wooden surface and the power cord extending outward.
